title: Basic
summary: Defines the critical input/output density weights, abstract GJMS operators, and operator families of order 2k.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the critical input/output density weights, abstract GJMS operators, and operator families of order 2k.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows GJMSoperators/Basic.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The critical input weight for the k-th GJMS construction. Mathematically, this is k - n / 2. In explicit terms, for n in the real numbers, and k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is k minus n divided by 2.

The output weight of the k-th GJMS construction. Mathematically, this is -k - n / 2. In explicit terms, for n in the real numbers, and k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is - k minus n divided by 2.
The output weight equals the input weight minus 2k. This encodes the fact that Δ̃^k lowers homogeneity by 2k. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,n\colon \mathbb{R},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\quad w_{\mathrm{out}} n k = w_{\mathrm{in}} n k - 2 \cdot k
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_eq_inputWeight_sub_two_mul (n : ℝ) (k : ℕ) : outputWeight n k = inputWeight n k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, input weight n 0 equals minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, output weight n 0 equals minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, input weight n 1 equals 1 minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 1 = 1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, output weight n 1 equals -1 minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 1 = -1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, input weight n 2 equals 2 minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 2 = 2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n in the real numbers, output weight n 2 equals -2 minus n divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 2 = -2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The critical input weight for the k-th GJMS operator associated to a fixed ambient calculus. In explicit terms, for k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is input weight the dimension n of the ambient calculus k.

The output weight for the k-th GJMS operator associated to a fixed ambient calculus. In explicit terms, for k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is output weight the dimension n of the ambient calculus k.
For k in the natural numbers, calculus Output Weight Cal Conf k equals calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k minus 2 times k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem calculusOutputWeight_eq_calculusInputWeight_sub_two_mul (k : ℕ) : calculusOutputWeight CalConf k = calculusInputWeight CalConf k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: calculus Input Weight Cal Conf 0 equals minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
calculus Output Weight Cal Conf 0 equals minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
calculus Input Weight Cal Conf 1 equals 1 minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
calculus Output Weight Cal Conf 1 equals -1 minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
calculus Input Weight Cal Conf 2 equals 2 minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
calculus Output Weight Cal Conf 2 equals -2 minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.

An abstract GJMS-type operator of order 2k. At the current abstraction level, a GJMS operator is represented by a weighted tangential ambient operator whose input and output weights are the standard GJMS weights: k - n / 2 → -k - n / 2. The actual construction from Δ̃^k is not included in this file. Its mathematical data are: k: the natural numbers; to Weighted Tangential Operator: Cal Conf.Weighted Tangential Operator; input Weight eq: to Weighted Tangential Operator.input weight equals calculus Input Weight Cal Conf k; output Weight eq: to Weighted Tangential Operator.output weight equals calculus Output Weight Cal Conf k.
The expected differential order of the GJMS operator. In explicit terms, for p of type abstract operator cal conf, its defining expression is 2 times P.k.
The input weight of an abstract GJMS operator. In explicit terms, for p of type abstract operator cal conf, its defining expression is P.to Weighted Tangential Operator.input weight.
The output weight of an abstract GJMS operator. In explicit terms, for p of type abstract operator cal conf, its defining expression is P.to Weighted Tangential Operator.output weight.
The underlying ambient operator. In explicit terms, for p of type abstract operator cal conf, its defining expression is P.to Weighted Tangential Operator.to Operator.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : Operator (Conf := Conf)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The underlying ambient operator maps the correct input weight to the output weight. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeight (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : CalConf.MapsHomogeneousWeight P.toOperator P.inputWeightOf P.outputWeightOf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The underlying ambient operator is tangential at its input weight. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem tangential (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight P.toOperator P.inputWeightOf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The input weight is explicitly k - n / 2. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeightOf_eq (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.inputWeightOf = calculusInputWeight CalConf P.k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The output weight is explicitly -k - n / 2. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeightOf_eq (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.outputWeightOf = calculusOutputWeight CalConf P.k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The output weight equals the input weight minus 2k. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeightOf_eq_inputWeightOf_sub_two_mul (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.outputWeightOf = P.inputWeightOf - 2 * (P.k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A formal family of GJMS operators, one for each natural number k. This is useful later when we want to package the whole GJMS sequence. Its mathematical data are: operator: the natural numbers maps to Abstract Operator Cal Conf; operator k: for every k : the natural numbers, operator k.k equals k.
The k-th GJMS operator in a family. In explicit terms, for p of type family cal conf, and k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is P.operator k.
correspondence.leanType: def get (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For P of type Family Cal Conf, and k in the natural numbers, P.get k.k equals k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem get_k (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : (P.get k).k = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The expected differential order of the k-th operator is 2k. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem get_order (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : (P.get k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zeroth GJMS operator, formally of order 0. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and p of type family cal conf, its defining expression is P.get 0.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P0 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first nontrivial GJMS operator, formally of order 2. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and p of type family cal conf, its defining expression is P.get 1.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P2 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The second nontrivial GJMS operator, formally of order 4. In explicit terms, for calconf of type calculus conf, and p of type family cal conf, its defining expression is P.get 2.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P4 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For P of type Family Cal Conf, P0 P.k equals 0.
For P of type Family Cal Conf, P2 P.k equals 1.
For P of type Family Cal Conf, P4 P.k equals 2.
For P of type Family Cal Conf, P0 P.order equals 0.
For P of type Family Cal Conf, P2 P.order equals 2.
For P of type Family Cal Conf, P4 P.order equals 4.

references.note: Original construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian.

references.locator: Ambient Laplacian and homogeneous extension discussion
references.note: Ambient-metric background for the formal construction.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
